notes: LEED Gold certified, liquid cooling designed from ground up, LTC systems 25% more efficient than air, WUE 0.203. GPU-as-a-Service onboarding in Montreal.

notes: 27MW greenfield with closed-loop chilled water (zero water consumption). Toronto Green Standard compliant. Mid-2026 go-live.

pue_source: Montreal II, Canada Data Center Campus
notes: Design PUE 1.2 at full capacity, free cooling most of year. Air-cooled currently — strong retrofit candidate for liquid cooling as AI tenants demand higher density.

notes: 5MW planned AI fabric site, liquid-cooled design consistent with Kamloops facility. Scalable to 20MW. Part of Bell's 500MW BC supercluster.
TRIPLE MATCH: PUE <1.17, 9MW, Vertiv CoolPhase Flex liquid+air hybrid deploying Q1 2025. KyotoCooling + free cooling 90% of year. 100% Hydro-Quebec renewable.
PUE as low as 1.15 with KyotoCooling. 30MW across 9 halls. Free cooling 90% of year. Vertiv liquid cooling partnership. 100% renewable.
46MW, PUE 1.25. Air-cooled. No target metrics matched but DLC offering available in 170+ DCs globally.
16MW, Smardt mag-lev chillers, water-side economizer. No liquid cooling yet. Equinix trialing liquid pods globally — TR2 could be a future deployment.

address: 34 Highland Park Way NE, Airdrie, AB
notes: 7.5MW small colo (former Rogers). ≤10MW = SAM target. No liquid cooling yet — retrofit opportunity. Part of 9-DC portfolio (49MW total).
400MW planned AI hub. 4.5GW total Alberta pipeline. Stantec engineering. Construction 2025. Cooling specs not yet disclosed — early opportunity for Solar Steam.
8MW Phase 1 (56MW campus). DLC up to 100kW/rack + immersion up to 300kW/rack. Canadian AI-ready.
$250M sovereign AI hub. DLC + immersion + heat recovery. Hypertec TRIDENT iG: 6 GPUs/2U, 200 GPUs/tank.
7.2MW (qualifies!). Liquid cooling planned for 150+ kW/rack GPU racks. Canadian sovereign AI.
LARGEST TWO-PHASE DLC DEPLOYMENT. 300MW. Accelsius NeuCool + AquaFlow immersion + Johnson Controls. 2026-2027.
Canada's largest AI DC. Bell + Saskatchewan gov. Construction spring 2026. Cooling decisions being made NOW.
1GW proposed with closed-loop liquid cooling. Community pushback on water — liquid cooling is the selling point. Very early pipeline.
$725M. CoreWeave building (DLC standard 130kW/rack). First ISED recipient ($240M). Sovereign AI.
5MW (qualifies!). GPUaaS. LTC deployed. BC hydro. Cologix $1.5B expansion.

CAD$19B total. 4+ Quebec sites. Fairwater zero-water DLC. Carbon negative by 2030.
30MW. 756 GPUs (H100+H200). Preparing liquid cooling. Heat to pool factory.
Upgrading to DTC liquid cooling (Jan 2025). Crypto→AI pivot.
Multi-billion. 600kW/rack DLC. Rubin Ultra-ready. Heat to greenhouse.
Cerebras CS-3 Wafer-Scale in Canada. First wafer-scale inference CA.

PUE 1.09 in summer with patented water-cooling since 2003, no chillers or mechanical cooling. 6 halls (BHS1-BHS6), ~90,000 servers with capacity for 3x more. Adjacent to Hydro-Quebec 1,903MW dam. WUE below 0.2l/kWh vs 1.8l/kWh industry average.

2MW facility with OVHcloud proprietary water-cooling technology. 1,000 sqm, 10,000 server capacity. Opened March 2024. $145M CAD investment in Toronto area. Qualifies on both capacity (<=10MW) and liquid cooling.

company: University of Waterloo / SHARCNET / Digital Research Alliance of Canada
notes: Canada's first immersion-cooled supercomputer. 9 single-phase immersion tanks + direct-to-chip liquid cooling for GPU subsystems. 752 nodes, 134,400 CPU cores, 288 NVIDIA H100 GPUs. Waste heat recycled to warm adjacent Quantum-Nano Centre. $50M system.
PUE 1.15 using free air cooling and hot/cold aisle containment. 10,000 sq ft within 790,000 sq ft complex. 600 tons N+1 cooling capacity. 99% renewable hydro power. Commissioned 2021.

7.5MW facility qualifies on capacity (<=10MW). Carrier neutral colocation in downtown Winnipeg. Powered by Manitoba Hydro (98%+ hydroelectric). 1,100 tonnes cooling capacity. 172,147 sqft building footprint.

PUE 1.20, 3.5MW, Uptime Tier III design. Chilled water system supports ultra-high density up to 100kW/cabinet for GPU workloads. 2N redundancy all systems. Qualifies on PUE (1.20) and capacity (<=10MW).
1MW small facility qualifies on capacity (<=10MW). Tier I with N+1/2N cooling redundancy. 15,340 sqft total, 7,500 sqft raised floor. Ideal candidate for liquid cooling retrofit.

1MW facility qualifies on capacity (<=10MW). Chilled water supports high density >30kW/cabinet. Downtown Vancouver carrier hotel. Hydroelectric power. Small footprint ideal for liquid cooling retrofit.

9MW high-density liquid-cooled facility with PUE 1.20. Qualifies on PUE (1.20), liquid cooling, AND capacity (<=10MW). Chilled-water + free cooling. Rapid deployment: 1-3MW in <150 days. Uptime Tier 3.

6MW facility qualifies on capacity (<=10MW). Uptime Tier III certified (design + construction). 100,923 sqft purpose-built. 568 tons N+1 cooling. Brookfield Infrastructure rebranded from Cyxtera/Evoque 2024.
2.7MW Tier III facility qualifies on capacity (<=10MW). Chilled water cooling, N+1 redundancy. 4,900 sqft. SOC2 Type 2, ASHRAE compliant. Crown corporation operator.
PUE as low as 1.1, 1.5MW facility. Qualifies on BOTH PUE (<=1.20) and capacity (<=10MW). 28,000 sqft, 7,000 sqft raised floor. N+1 power and cooling redundancy. Up to 20kW/rack.

850-acre AI/HPC site 20km south of Pincher Creek. Initial 100MW natural gas, scalable to 4GW. Targeting ~130MW by end 2026. Captus Energy acquisition CAD$27M. Carbon capture planned. Pipeline opportunity.

company: AVAIO Digital Partners / ADAM Real Estate (JV)
notes: Scalable to 50MW sustainable hyperscale facility. Between Ottawa and Montreal. Serves federal/private demand in capital region. 100% Quebec hydro power. Pipeline opportunity for cooling vendor engagement.

54MW hyperscale expansion under construction. C$225M CPP/Deutsche Bank financing. Pre-leased to GPU-focused AI cloud compute provider. High probability of liquid cooling given AI/GPU focus. Pipeline opportunity.

company: Simon Fraser University / Digital Research Alliance of Canada
notes: Canada's fastest academic supercomputer. CA$80M investment replacing Cedar. 165,888 cores, 640 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, 50PB storage, 19.96 PF peak. Transitioned from air cooling (Cedar) to direct-to-chip liquid cooling. PUE 1.07 inherited from Cedar facility design. TOP500 #78. Powered by clean BC hydro energy.

company: University of Toronto / SciNet / Digital Research Alliance of Canada
notes: $52M replacement for Niagara supercomputer. 241,056 cores (AMD Turin), 252 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, 29PB VAST NVMe storage. 1,700 kW power consumption. Water-cooled with free-air cooling integration. Infrastructure replacement included new plumbing for cooling systems. Launched August 2025.

company: Calcul Quebec / ETS / Digital Research Alliance of Canada
notes: New supercomputer replacing Narval at ETS Montreal. 136,896 cores, 324 GPUs. TOP500 #192 (GPU), #242 (CPU). Green500 #73. Launched June 2025. Cooling type not publicly disclosed -- Green500 ranking suggests reasonable efficiency. Potential liquid cooling upgrade candidate.

300MW+ campus scaling to 1GW in Regina, SK. Closed-loop immersion cooling by CoolIT Systems. PUE target 1.05-1.15. WUE near-zero. 7,000+ free cooling hours/year. Islanded micro-grid with gas turbines + renewables + 100MWh battery. Phase 1 (2026-27): 10MW IT, $300M CapEx. 100% Canadian-owned. Bird Construction, TransGas, SaskTel, FNPA partners.

500kW across 3 data halls, 20+ customers. Acquired from Carbon60 in March 2026 for $1.6M + $150K deferred. Liquid cooling configs supported up to 150kW/rack. Protected B security clearance ready. SSAE16 SOC2 Type2 compliant. First DC in Atlantic Canada for Carrier Connect (5th overall). Small facility in underserved market.

Proposed 380MW AI data center on 120-hectare Spruce Lake Industrial Park site. $2B total investment. 190MW on-site gas-fired plant (VoltaGrid) + 190MW from NB Power grid (from 2028). Pending EIA approval. Significant community opposition over water use and emissions. Target operational early 2028.

100% Water Cooling System. 12,000 server capacity, 132 full racks. 700 tons AC capacity. 3.5MW diesel generator. Located at 5945 Couture Blvd, Saint-Leonard QC. Opened 2006, acquired by Leaseweb from iWeb in August 2021. Water-cooled facility in Montreal -- potential for cooling technology upgrade.

1,500 servers hosting public and private sector workloads. 195 Colonnade Road, Ottawa. Canadian sovereign cloud provider. Working to move off Ontario grid to natural gas cogeneration. Equivalent power of 10,000 residential homes. State-of-the-art cooling with redundant units. Global footprint across Canada, US, Bahamas, Australia, UK.

Tier 3 design carrier-neutral data center in Winkler, MB (115km SW of Winnipeg). Commscope NG4 cross-connect system. Proximity to US border with diverse redundant crossing points. Manitoba's low-cost hydro power. Small facility in underserved rural Manitoba market.

7.5MW purpose-built facility. 64,000 sq ft. Uptime Institute Tier III design-certified. Built 2015 for Manitoba Telecom/Bell MTS, acquired by Equinix in $780M deal (13 Bell DCs) in 2020. Equinix rolling out direct-to-chip liquid cooling across 100+ DCs globally -- Winnipeg expansion planned.

2 colocation sites in Halifax, NS. Carrier neutral. Half/quarter/full rack leasing. Up to 100G+ connectivity. 20+ years operating history. Small operator in underserved Atlantic Canada market. Halifax ranked #2 Emerging Tech Market in North America (CBRE).

TIA-942 Tier III. N+1 redundant cooling. Dual-path environmental control (temp and humidity). Diesel backup generation. Located at 5562 Sackville Street, Halifax. Operated by Internetworking Atlantic Inc (IAI), now Rogers Carrier Services. Enterprise-grade facility in Atlantic Canada.

Scalable up to 24MW. 2N power, N+1 cooling. High-density rack ready up to 50kW/cabinet. Hot/cold aisle containment with free cold air cooling. Montreal location leverages Quebec climate for PUE efficiency. Canadian dedicated server and colocation provider.

4 data centers in downtown Winnipeg on private fibre network. Manages Manitoba Internet Exchange (MBix). Carrier neutral colocation. 100M/1G/10G connections. Largest DC operator in Manitoba by site count. Small server rooms in Exchange District.

2MW carrier-neutral data center at 390 March Road, Kanata ON. Supports free-air, hot-aisle containment, and liquid cooling configs up to 150kW/rack. Protected B security clearance ready. Part of Carrier Connect's 5-DC national portfolio. Ottawa government/tech hub location.

Vancouver-based cleantech startup. Patent-pending closed-loop liquid-cooling using phase change materials (PCM) and AI-driven energy optimization. 80%+ cooling energy savings. Zero water waste. SuperPODs with proprietary passive PCM cooling compatible with immersion, DLC, or air-to-liquid. No chillers or refrigerants needed. Strategy: retrofit existing halls then build net-zero GPU farms.

200,000 sq ft data center proposed in Nanaimo, BC (Vancouver Island). Operator runs similar facility in Hong Kong. Committed to BC Hydro: no data/crypto mining. Potential 70,000L/day water usage for cooling. No subsea cables to Nanaimo currently -- nearest is Google Topaz cable landing at Port Alberni (40km NW). Not AI-focused.

First Canadian colocation DC to implement large-scale fresh-air cooling. 231 Amber St, Markham ON. Completed 2015. Carrier neutral. Modular AC, transformers, UPS. Variable-speed fans and smart coils reduce fan energy 20-35%. Advocates for DLC and rear-door heat exchangers for high-density. Potential liquid cooling upgrade candidate.

facility_name: QScale Q01 — Quebec Sovereign AI Data Center (Liquid Cooled, 600kW/rack)
notes: Canada's first purpose-built AI colocation facility. 600kW+ per cabinet liquid cooling. 96MW campus (8 planned DCs). OCP Ready. Hydro-Quebec clean electricity. Heat recovery. Part of Canada's $925.6M sovereign AI compute strategy (Budget 2025). Government call for proposals (100MW+ sovereign AI DCs) ran Jan-Feb 2026. QScale is prime candidate for Canadian sovereign AI infrastructure.

facility_name: IREN Prince George BC -- 10MW Liquid-Cooled AI DC (GB300)
notes: 10MW IT load liquid-cooled AI data center at Prince George, BC. New building under construction for NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 systems (4,500+ GB300 GPUs). 12-acre freehold site. 100% renewable energy (BC Hydro, ~98% hydroelectric). Full fiber connectivity. Part of IREN's $9.7B Microsoft AI cloud contract. 50MW total site capacity. Bitcoin mining to AI cloud transition.

facility_name: Bit Digital Enovum MTL1 -- 24MW HPC DC (Montreal)
notes: 24MW Tier 3 HPC data center at 3195 Chemin Bedford, Montreal. 70,000 sq ft. Enovum acquired by Bit Digital in 2024 as part of vertical integration strategy. 288MW proprietary pipeline across major metro areas. All future Enovum sites designed for DTC liquid cooling. Part of Bit Digital's pivot from crypto mining to HPC infrastructure.

Canada's first immersion-cooled supercomputer. $50M system. 752 nodes, 134,400 Intel Xeon 6 cores (Granite Rapids AP -- first major HPC deployment), 288 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, 25 PB flash storage. 9 single-phase immersion tanks + DLC for GPUs. Heat recovered to warm adjacent Lazaridis Quantum-Nano Centre. Built by Nokia/Hypertec. Replaces Graham system.

facility_name: Equinix TR5 Markham -- Waste Heat to 14M sqft Mixed-Use Development (MDE)
notes: Heat exported from Equinix TR5 is distributed via Markham District Energy (MDE, est. 2000) to over 14 million sqft of mixed-use development including residential buildings, a hotel, university, and local shopping center in downtown Markham. MDE heat pump project commissioned late 2023. MDE was established by City of Markham after 1998 ice storm. IBM Canada was first anchor customer. Excess heat must be rejected in summer when demand is low.

facility_name: Telehouse / Enwave Toronto (151 + 250 Front St) -- Deep Lake Water Cooling + Heat Recovery
notes: Telehouse Canada operates DCs at 151 and 250 Front Street West in Toronto, cooled by Enwave's Deep Lake Water Cooling System (DLWC). Three intake pipes draw 4C water from Lake Ontario to cool ~190 buildings including data centers, hospitals, and campuses. Waste heat from DCs is leveraged by Enwave's Green Heat Plant (Pearl Street) -- an electrified heat pump upgrades waste heat into district heating hot water. Saves Telehouse water, electricity, and infrastructure costs. Part of North America's largest district cooling system.
